exercise_title: Unit 1: Past Tenses & Used to / Would

content.meaning:
1. action completed in the past when there is direct or indirect time reference
+ e.g. He left an hour ago. (direct time reference)
+ e.g. She phoned before the boss came. (indirect time reference)
2. past habitual action
+ e.g. He travelled/used to travel a lot when he was young.
3. past actions happening one after the other
+ e.g. He stood up, got his briefcase and left the office.
4. past action which won't be repeated
+ e.g. Monroe starred in "The Seven Year Itch."

1. past action in progress at a given point in time.
+ e.g. She was still working at eight o'clock yesterday evening.
2. past action in progress interrupted by another action.
+ e.g. She was leaving when the phone rang.
3. simultaneous past actions
+ e.g. While Jane was getting dressed, Tom was enjoying his drink.
4. polite inquiries
+ e.g. I was wondering if you could help me.

1. past action which happened before another past action
+ e.g. She had already typed all the letters before her boss arrived.
2. as the past equivalent of the Present Perfect
+ Compare: I haven't done it yet. I had already done it by the time he called.

1. duration of a past action occurring before another past action
+ e.g. She had been cooking all day long when Tom came home with some fish and chips.
2. action producing visible results in the past
+ e.g. She was covered in paint because she had been painting her room.

(past habitual action)
+ e.g. He used to work till late at night. (He doesn't anymore.)

(habitual action)
+ e.g. She isn't used to driving on the left.
+ e.g. I haven't got used to living abroad yet.

(past repeated action and routine)
+ e.g. When I was at my grandparents' cottage I would wake up early and go for a ride.
